---
title: "Wetland Assessment Report"
subtitle: "`r params$project_name`"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  word_document:
    reference_docx: "report_style.docx"
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
params:
  project_id:   "PROJECT_ID"
  project_name: "Project Name"
  client:       "Client Name"
  prepared_by:  "Green Plan Ltd."
  figure_dir:   "reports/figures"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(here)
```

# 1. Introduction

*[Insert project background and regulatory context here.]*

# 2. Methods

## 2.1 Vegetation Assessment

Field vegetation data were collected using standard plot-based methodology and classified
according to the Wetland Assessment and Impact Rating (WAIR) framework and the Alberta
Native Plant Council (ANPC) Wetland Plant Communities classification system.

## 2.2 Soils Assessment

Soil profiles were described and classified following the Canadian System of Soil
Classification (CSSC). Peat layers were characterized using the Von Post humification scale.

## 2.3 Water Permanence

Historical water permanence was assessed from multi-date satellite imagery using NDWI
pixel classification, supplemented by LiDAR-derived topographic analysis.

# 3. Results

## 3.1 Vegetation

```{r fig-upl-obl, fig.cap="Figure 1. Wetland indicator cover by plot."}
knitr::include_graphics(here(params$figure_dir, "fig_upl_obl_by_plot.png"))
```

```{r fig-invasive, fig.cap="Figure 2. Native vs invasive cover by plot."}
knitr::include_graphics(here(params$figure_dir, "fig_native_invasive_by_plot.png"))
```

```{r fig-noxious, fig.cap="Figure 3. Noxious weed species heat map."}
knitr::include_graphics(here(params$figure_dir, "fig_noxious_heatmap.png"))
```

## 3.2 Soils

```{r fig-peat, fig.cap="Figure 4. Peat profiles by Von Post humification."}
knitr::include_graphics(here(params$figure_dir, "fig_peat_vonpost.png"))
```

## 3.3 Water Permanence

*[Insert water permanence figures and interpretation here.]*

# 4. Wetland Classification

*[Insert classification table and narrative.]*

# 5. Conclusions

*[Insert regulatory conclusions.]*

---

*Report prepared by `r params$prepared_by` for `r params$client`.*
